title: Repair RepoGround runtime false-green health and content-search contracts
goal: Fix the two runtime contract defects exposed by T025: the repository launcher probes /health although the API contract exposes /api/health, and retrieval artifacts populate path tokens while leaving FTS content empty so content queries silently return no hits.

acceptance.assertion: The canonical launcher and service contract use one tested health endpoint; a started service passes the launcher probe and an unavailable or wrong-version service fails closed.
A retrieval build from a fixture with source text writes non-empty searchable content rows, not only path_tokens, and preserves deterministic metadata.
A query for a symbol or phrase present only in file content returns the expected file; a path-only control remains functional and a missing phrase returns zero hits.
Regression tests fail against the pre-fix /health mismatch and empty-content artifact, and verify negative authentication, traversal and stale-index semantics.
Existing RepoGround CLI, MCP, release and legacy compatibility tests remain green; historical schemas and names are not rewritten.
The exact reviewed diff passes full local tests and GitHub CI, is merged, and is verified through a parallel runtime probe before production deployment.

rollback.trigger: Health probes accept an unhealthy service, retrieval output changes incompatibly, content indexing causes unacceptable size or latency, or existing path queries regress.
rollback.procedure: Keep the production runtime on d54a3f3d, revert the source change, preserve failing fixture artifacts and query receipts, and leave this task blocked until a bounded replacement passes both path and content probes.

conditions: a foreign RepoGround writer overlaps the intended files or worktree
the current main head differs from the recorded baseline before branch creation
the content source cannot be identified without changing public artifact semantics
the fix would require rewriting historical bundles or compatibility schemas

metadata.block_reason: RepoGround PR #1041 is merged and all source gates passed, but the mandatory parallel runtime probe, controlled deployment and live readback cannot be executed in this session because the installed Grabowski app has no registered tool namespace.
metadata.next_action: With a registered Grabowski host connector, build an immutable runtime from merge commit 7777f6fd690e504e11e26fbaa4ab5c4d2fc4a637, probe it in parallel, verify /api/health and content-only retrieval, prove rollback, then deploy and close T006 only after live readback.

validated_acceptance.health-contract: PASS for source contract and regression tests; live started-service proof pending
validated_acceptance.content-query: PASS for content-only, path-only and missing-token controls
validated_acceptance.no-false-green: PASS for source regression suite and existing negative controls
validated_acceptance.compatibility: PASS in full GitHub CI and deterministic release build
validated_acceptance.published-proof: BLOCKED after merge: parallel runtime probe, production deployment and live readback pending

does_not_establish: current production runtime state after the last host readback
permission to alter the running production service without a new parallel proof
